what_it_does: A standalone @dsCard preview (group 'Components') showing the brand's rounded content box in five variants — media box, content box, emphasis box, compact stat box, and pill — each rendered from the same `.box` treatment differing only in the tokens it references; media boxes use a draggable <image-slot> and text boxes are contenteditable so the card is interactively fillable.
observations: Comment header `@dsCard group="Components" name="Card / box variants" ... viewport="900x460"`.
Links three stylesheets (../colors_and_type.css, ../tokens/controls.css, _card.css) and a script `image-slot.js` providing the <image-slot> custom element.

One `.box` base: 1.5px --accent-gold border, --r-lg radius, a layered linear-gradient paper sheen (--bg-surface/--paper/--paper-2), --shadow-card; variants are modifier classes (.emphasis, .md, .pill).
.emphasis swaps the fill to a gold wash (--accent-gold-50/--accent-gold-soft); .md uses --r-md; .pill uses --r-pill.
Text elements are contenteditable with a gold focus ring (inset box-shadow --accent-gold-soft).
<image-slot> instances carry shape/radius/src/placeholder attributes and a sample staged-bedroom illustration.
Lead and foot text explicitly enumerate the tokens each variant references (radius --r-md/--r-lg/--r-pill, fill --paper vs --accent-gold-50, edge --accent-gold, shadow --shadow-card).
Lead cross-references sibling cards: entity marks live in 'Entity shape system', the staged ladder in 'Staged-process flow' — this card is just the box.
standouts: Embodies the core single-source doctrine: one box treatment, variants differ ONLY by the tokens referenced — no second box implementation.
Interactive/fillable card (drag image, click-to-edit text) rather than a static specimen.
Uses a custom <image-slot> web component for the media variant.
conventions: Strongly adheres to the project's no-literals rule — colours, radii, shadow all via var() tokens; almost no hardcoded values in the box itself.
Cross-references other cards by name rather than duplicating their content (anti-drift).
notes: A components card proving 'one rounded content box, variants are just different token values' — interactively fillable via image-slot and contenteditable.
questions: What does image-slot.js implement for the <image-slot> element (drag/drop, sizing), since the card depends on it for the media variant?
Are --paper / --paper-2 / --accent-gold-deep defined in the linked stylesheets (referenced here but not defined)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata says 'the rounded content box in its variants ... radius/fill/shadow from tokens' and the body demonstrates exactly that, token-driven and fillable.
apparent_themes: One treatment, many variants differing only by referenced tokens (single-source / anti-drift)
Tokenised box: edge, fill, radius, shadow all from variables
Cross-referencing sibling cards instead of duplicating
intention_signals: Aims to demonstrate the brand's fundamental container as a single token-parameterised box, showing that visual variety comes from token values not new components, and to let the viewer fill it to see real content.
novelty: The interactive fillable specimen (drag-image + contenteditable) and the explicit 'variants differ only in tokens' proof, including a custom <image-slot> element.
contribution: Contributes the canonical content-box component (one gold-edged paper-sheen rounded box with media/content/emphasis/compact/pill variants, all token-driven and fillable) that a fused system would keep as its core container primitive.
summary_for_embedding: A Components-group design-system preview card showing the brand's single rounded content box in five token-driven variants (media, content, emphasis, compact, pill), interactively fillable via an image-slot element and contenteditable text.
intention_for_embedding: To demonstrate the core content-box container as one token-parameterised treatment whose variants differ only by referenced tokens, and to let viewers fill it with real content.
